text: Diffusion magnetic resonance imaging (dMRI) has become a popular tool in both research and clinical fields for in-vivo brain studies. Although many image processing tools have been developed, the fully automatic processing of dMRI data is still a challenging task. In this study, we present a MATLAB toolbox named “Pipeline for Analyzing braiN Diffusion imAges” (PANDA) that can be used for fully automatic brain diffusion images processing. Some processing modules from FMRIB Software Library (FSL), Pipeline System for Octave and Matlab (PSOM), Diffusion Toolkit and MRIcron were integrated into PANDA. By using any number of diffusion raw data of different subjects stored in DICOM or NIfTI format, PANDA can automatically perform a series of processing from DICOM/NIfTI to diffusion metrics (such as fractional anisotropy (FA) and mean diffusivity (MD)) that are ready for voxel-wise, atlas-based and Tract-Based Spatial Statistics (TBSS) analysis, as well as for the construction of anatomical brain networks for all subjects. The PANDA can utilize multiple cores of a computer to process different subjects in parallel, which can greatly save time when dealing with large number of data sets. In addition, the user-friendly graphical user interface (GUI) of PANDA allows users to be interactive and to adjust the input/output settings and processing parameters. PANDA is an open-source tool and is available at http://www.nitrc.org/projects/panda/.
